taskCaption: drawer articulation increases to the RLBench-calibrated open extension (articulation-event diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Asserts, via the FROZEN matcher: terminal drawer extension within tolerance of the RLBench-calibrated 0.234 m (one hard ARTICULATION_GOAL -> goal_satisfaction, numeric); the drawer's articulation INCREASED rather than stayed flat (articulation_transitions -- DIRECTION only); and one ARTICULATION_CHANGE event is present (event_presence). One step beyond the value-only target: it requires an articulation INCREASE to the calibrated value plus the change event, not only the terminal value. See enforcedVsAuthoring for what is NOT enforced.
addedOverValueOnly: objectStates(authored initial 0.0 + terminal 0.234) -> contributes the increase DIRECTION
articulationTransition(authored 0.0->0.234) -> contributes the increase DIRECTION only
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: The frozen matcher (csg.matcher._probe_articulation_transitions) reduces every articulation transition to (object, jointKind, valueKind, INCREASE/DECREASE/FLAT) -- direction only, numeric endpoints discarded -- and the only numeric articulation check (goal_satisfaction) reads the robot's TERMINAL value, never the initial. ENFORCED: terminal value within tol of 0.234, an INCREASE direction, and the ARTICULATION_CHANGE event's presence. AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): the numeric initial 0.0 and the fromState/toState/objectState magnitudes. A trace that starts at e.g. 0.10 and increases to 0.234 also PASSes (tests/test_rlbench_articulation_event.py::test_articulation_event_does_not_pin_initial_value).
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: Deliberately NO contact, handle-contact, CONTACT_BEGIN event, temporal edge, or strict contact-before-motion order. The single ARTICULATION_CHANGE event carries event_presence + articulation_transitions support but ZERO event-order pair support (there is no second event to order against), so event_order stays vacuous. The adapter has no honest RLBench evidence for who/what caused the motion; asserting contact or order would be unevidenced. Those remain a later contact/order target.
pilotMetadata.source: RLBench OpenDrawer (bottom/middle/top), live Runpod rerun 2026-06-14; terminal extension min 0.2347 / mean 0.2356 / max 0.2362 m across 9 fresh demos. See pilots/rlbench/fixtures/live_runpod_20260614_rerun/.
pilotMetadata.toleranceNote: Intended/documentary only and NOT read by the verifier. The FROZEN matcher enforces a single global MatcherConfig.articulation_tol (0.05 m), not this per-target field; the increase (terminal minus initial) far exceeds the 0.05 m tolerance so the transition registers as INCREASE, and the terminal value is within tolerance of the 0.234 goal. csg.canon ignores unknown top-level keys, so this block never enters any probe.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. It asserts the drawer's articulation INCREASED to the calibrated extension and that a change event is present; it does NOT pin the initial value, nor assert the agent contacted or caused the motion. Read it for articulation-event mappability, not as a full open_drawer acceptance test.
